/works/coca-cola.title: COCA-COLA - My Framer Site
/works/coca-cola.description: Discover YUYA, a minimalist Framer template designed for creatives, designers, developers, and other professionals who appreciate simplicity, elegance, and intuitive design.
h4: Curious about what we can create together? Let’s bring something extraordinary to life!
p: Strategic reframing: Shifted the engagement from a training site to a full capability ecosystem tied to sales KPIs
Autonomous leadership: Acted as the glue between product, AI, field research, and executive vision — no line management required
AI integration: Embedded Microsoft Copilot into the sales learning journey, driving interest from other CPG clients
Business outcome focus: Connected learning design to sales performance, onboarding speed, and manager effectiveness at scale

Coca-Cola Hellenic operates across 29 countries, spanning Europe, Asia, and Africa. Its frontline salesforce — known as Business Developers (BDs) — are responsible for perfect execution in retail outlets, directly influencing performance and brand visibility.

Faced with a costly 6-month onboarding cycle and high attrition, CCH partnered with Microsoft to reimagine sales capability development. The result: an immersive Sales Academy powered by AI helping reps learn faster, engage better, and feel more confident in the field.
Traditional onboarding relied on slide decks, dense PDFs, and in-market shadowing
Learning timelines stretched to 6 months, with inconsistent training quality across regions
Existing tools lacked engagement, adaptability, or measurable learning outcomes
CCH needed a scalable, modern solution that could run on everyday iPads or optional
We began with a hypothesis: that combining spatial learning with an AI coach could cut onboarding time by a third while improving engagement.
Built a VR experience featuring Hellen, an AI coach trained on CCH’s learning materials
Users practiced product placement with real-time feedback in a virtual retail space
Launched at CCH’s global summit in Cairo — tested by 300+ sales reps
Designed three immersive outlets: supermarket, café, bar
Delivered as both iPad app and VR experience for scalable rollout
Integrated Hellen using Azure OpenAI for contextual coaching
Ensured alignment with Responsible AI principles
Prototyped in Unity, Figma, and Gravity Sketch
Refined through mixed-reality testing in Prague and other EU markets
Framed Hellen as a sales performance coach, not a chatbot — focused on guidance, not just answers
Defined her role in the learning journey, from onboarding to real-time coaching and manager handoffs
Shaped personality to be confident, supportive, and context-aware, based on CCH’s sales tone
Designed interaction flows across key scenarios (e.g. feedback, guidance, reinforcement)
Co-developed domain-specific intents with data science using real training materials
Tested prompts with BDs to ensure clarity, motivation, and credibility
Built a modular prompt library to scale across markets and SKUs
Embedded Responsible AI practices to ensure fairness, transparency, and safety
10,000+ learners across Serbia, Romania, Czech Republic, Ireland, and Nigeria
Reps reported higher confidence, better product understanding, and improved real-world execution
AI coach Hellen became a central part of CCH’s digital learning strategy
Positioned Microsoft as a trusted innovation partner in frontline skilling
The project is now cited internally as a blueprint for future AI + spatial learning engagements
You’ve helped us reimagine how we train our most important people — the ones in front of customers every day. This has global potential.

/works/aerospace-ai.title: Aerospace AI - My Framer Site
/works/aerospace-ai.description: Discover YUYA, a minimalist Framer template designed for creatives, designers, developers, and other professionals who appreciate simplicity, elegance, and intuitive design.
h4: Curious about what we can create together? Let’s bring something extraordinary to life!
p: Defined an AI product vision for complex aerospace operations where reliability, traceability, and speed are critical.
Translated dense operational workflows into a clear assistant model for planning, monitoring, and decision support.
Created interaction patterns that make AI recommendations explainable, reviewable, and safe to act on.

Aerospace teams operate in environments where small delays and unclear information can create major downstream impact. The opportunity was to explore how AI could help teams interpret complex signals, coordinate faster, and make confident decisions under pressure.
The challenge was to design an AI experience that felt powerful without becoming opaque: every recommendation needed context, source visibility, and an obvious path to human review.
Reduce time spent searching across operational data, reports, and status updates.
Support decision-making without hiding uncertainty or accountability.
Create a system that could scale from individual workflows to cross-team operations.
Mapped planning, monitoring, escalation, and reporting workflows to identify where AI could safely reduce friction.
Separated moments that needed automation from moments that required human judgment and review.
Designed AI outputs around source references, confidence indicators, assumptions, and recommended next actions.
Created patterns for exception handling, escalation, and handoff when the system could not provide a reliable answer.
Defined reusable components for assistant prompts, operational summaries, risk states, and activity history.
Balanced executive visibility with practitioner-level detail so the same system could support different altitudes of work.
The operational vision framed Aerospace AI as a command layer for complex environments: a way to read signals, surface risk, recommend actions, and keep people in control.
The experience showed how AI could become a trusted partner for planning and response, not just a faster search box.
Created a clear AI product direction for aerospace operations and decision intelligence.
Established reusable UX patterns for explainability, confidence, escalation, and human-in-the-loop review.
Provided a foundation for stakeholder demos, pilot planning, and future operational prototypes.
This work made the potential of AI feel operationally credible: clear, explainable, and designed for real-world decision-making.

/works/dxb-ai.title: DXB AI - My Framer Site
/works/dxb-ai.description: Discover YUYA, a minimalist Framer template designed for creatives, designers, developers, and other professionals who appreciate simplicity, elegance, and intuitive design.
h4: Curious about what we can create together? Let’s bring something extraordinary to life!
p: Shaped an AI-first case-study experience around citizen services, operational intelligence, and executive decision-making.
Mapped complex service workflows into clear assistant patterns that could scale across departments.
Created a trust-led UX model for AI responses, handoffs, data confidence, and human review.

DXB AI needed to make a broad set of city, customer, and operational services feel simple, reliable, and immediately useful. The challenge was not just adding AI to an interface; it was designing a service layer people could trust.

Teams needed a way to move from scattered processes and fragmented knowledge toward a single experience that could answer questions, guide tasks, and surface the right next action without hiding uncertainty.
Define the clearest entry points for AI-assisted services.
Design confidence, escalation, and auditability into every response.
Create a platform model that could support multiple departments without becoming generic.
Mapped user journeys across request intake, status tracking, decision support, and service resolution.
Identified where AI could reduce friction without removing necessary human judgment.
Designed conversational flows that clarify intent, summarize data, and provide transparent next steps.
Defined response states for confidence, missing information, escalation, and manual review.
Created reusable patterns for prompts, answer cards, source references, task handoffs, and feedback loops.
Balanced a premium executive feel with practical operational workflows for daily users.
The service vision positioned DXB AI as a trusted intelligence layer: a single place to ask, act, review, and learn across connected services.
The concept connected high-level strategy with everyday utility, showing how AI could support faster decisions while keeping accountability visible.
Created a clear product narrative for DXB AI across service access, operational support, and decision intelligence.
Defined reusable UX patterns for trustworthy AI interactions and service handoffs.
Provided a scalable foundation for future prototypes, stakeholder demos, and cross-department pilots.
The work gave us a tangible direction for how AI can become useful, trusted, and scalable across complex services.

/works/samsung-lu.title: SAMSUNG - My Framer Site
/works/samsung-lu.description: Discover YUYA, a minimalist Framer template designed for creatives, designers, developers, and other professionals who appreciate simplicity, elegance, and intuitive design.
h4: Curious about what we can create together? Let’s bring something extraordinary to life!
p: Strategic autonomy: Defined the business goal (replace live events with measurable virtual engagement), pitched the Unreal‑engine route, and won approval by demonstrating incremental prototyping ROI.
Cross-discipline leadership: Unified film production, spatial audio, real-time tech, UX, and CRM under a single cohesive experience strategy.
Decision leverage: Introduced gamification and e‑com tracking, tied live metrics to commercial outcomes—without direct manager oversight.
Business outcome focus: Showcased impact via increased dwell, session completion, product clicks, media reach, and awards—not just visuals.

In response to COVID restrictions on in-person events, Samsung commissioned a first-of-its-kind virtual launch using Unreal Engine. The result: a hyper-realistic, interactive 3D “Samsung House” showcasing their ecosystem—mobile, wearables, TV, audio, appliances—with embedded live hosts, AR moments, and first-time-to-market tech integrations.
Replace postponed physical events globally with an immersive digital equivalent.
Stand out amid webinar fatigue and low recall from typical virtual formats.
Integrate cross-product narratives (folding phones, projectors, vacuum robots, fridges) into a cohesive flow.
Enable real-time analytics, interaction data, and commerce-linked tracking across Europe.
Problem framing & scope: Shifted ambition beyond “virtual showroom” to an “e‑xperience”—a gamified, measurable, interactive narrative hub with live presenters and shoppable hotspots.
Stakeholder alignment: Led UX workshops across Samsung, tech (Unreal), AR, audio, video production—to agree on content flow, technical feasibility, and business objectives.
Tech-first prototyping: Piloted ray tracing for realism, pixel streaming for consistent performance across devices, and spatial 8D audio.

Interactive narrative design: Designed six themed “rooms” each centered on a product vignette (e.g., modular fridge, Neo QLED TV, Bespoke Jet vacuum). Embedded navigable filmic moments plus user-triggered deep dives to product content or e‑com links.
Engagement mechanics: Conceived a gamified Easter-egg hunt—finding collectible comic‑books unlocked a superhero mini-comic surprise—boosting dwell and exploration.
Post-event lifecycle: Structured drop-in on-demand access for partner deep-dive sessions, feeding CRM and supporting purchase decisions.
5k+ live attendees across Europe; 60% full-session completion of a 45‑minute show.
+29% dwell time compared to conventional video webcasts.
4k+ product clicks, enabling CRM-level attribution and measurable user journey paths.
Won multiple industry awards: Best B2B Experience (The Drum), Best Interactive Video, Brand Experience accolades.
Sparked a shift at Samsung toward “virtual-first” immersive product launches—confirmed by ongoing internal adoption and related teams. (inferred from broad coverage and awards)
By creating the ‘Life Unstoppable’ virtual world, we pushed the boundaries of virtual immersive experiences … bringing life, work and entertainment together using products that seamlessly connect

/works/equinor.title: EQUINOR - My Framer Site
/works/equinor.description: Discover YUYA, a minimalist Framer template designed for creatives, designers, developers, and other professionals who appreciate simplicity, elegance, and intuitive design.
h4: Curious about what we can create together? Let’s bring something extraordinary to life!
p: Led the UX vision and design execution for a complex, data-driven enterprise product
Balanced technical modelling, AI explainability, and user needs across functional roles
Prototyped, tested, and delivered interaction systems that went into real-world MVP code
Turned a design process into a strategic business accelerator—proving design as a growth enabler

To meet its net-zero targets, Equinor must expand from 1 GW to over 12 GW of offshore wind capacity by 2030. But the traditional site evaluation process—fragmented, manual, and painfully slow—wasn’t keeping up. The company needed a new platform to rapidly screen and model hundreds of potential wind farm sites each year.
Microsoft partnered with Equinor to co-design a digital tool enabling early-stage wind project teams to evaluate locations, simulate energy production (AEP), and submit better bids, faster.
3–4 months per bid: Equinor’s wind farm bids were built manually using siloed data, Excel sheets, and physical planning boards
Scaling impossible: Evaluating dozens of projects a year was hard enough—hundreds was out of reach
Missed opportunities: Competitive tenders required fast, accurate, data-backed decisions or risk being left behind
Low standardization: Institutional knowledge was spread across teams and tools, making repeatability difficult
Kicked off with a Design Thinking workshop to align on user goals and business outcomes
Reframed the problem as an AI-powered decision support platform for wind project leads
Used journey maps and storyboards to visualise how to replace the infamous 5m-long physical planning board
Built a map-based UI to select offshore areas and simulate wind energy metrics (AEP)
Integrated real models to generate early forecasts and scenarios for business case development
Prototypes included 3D turbine layouts, dashboards, and side-by-side comparisons
Conducted deep research with engineers, analysts, and non-technical stakeholders
Ran weekly feedback loops with Equinor’s internal team to validate flows and refine interaction
Used Microsoft’s HAX framework to guide trust, transparency, and explainability in AI features
Developed data visualization systems to help teams explain trade-offs to leadership
Designed for dual user groups: technical engineers and commercial decision-makers
Final UI integrated with Equinor’s Azure-based data platform
Created high-fidelity visual assets, UX documentation, and product architecture handoff
Co-engineered MVP in close collaboration with Equinor teams to ensure scalability and adoption
Reduced site evaluation time from 3–4 months to 3–4 weeks
~80% reduction in cycle time for investment-grade wind opportunity decisions
Standardized site analysis with consistent AEP models and reusable data sets
Enabled Equinor to screen dozens of sites in parallel, scaling pipeline without scaling headcount
Improved bid quality and confidence, enabling smarter portfolio decisions
Supported estimated $450M+ in potential engineering cost savings and value capture
Cemented Microsoft’s role as a strategic innovation partner, leading to follow-on AI collaborations
Create a free website with Framer, the website builder loved by startups, designers and agencies.

/works/bmw.title: BMW - My Framer Site
/works/bmw.description: Discover YUYA, a minimalist Framer template designed for creatives, designers, developers, and other professionals who appreciate simplicity, elegance, and intuitive design.
h4: Curious about what we can create together? Let’s bring something extraordinary to life!
p: Automotive – Engineering Systems, AI & Product Search
Drove the design and validation of a high-stakes AI product from discovery to delivery
Used systems thinking to connect user pain, AI capability, and enterprise constraints
Created a trusted user experience for AI in a domain where failure is expensive
Helped turn one project (3DC) into a platform—and a company-wide AI vision

BMW’s engineering teams work across thousands of parts, systems, and documents in vehicle design and manufacturing. But their internal search tools—spanning legacy databases and siloed platforms—made daily workflows painful and slow.

Engineers were spending 5 minutes to several hours searching for part data, compliance checks, or production histories—relying on outdated interfaces and tribal knowledge. Previous internal AI tools had failed due to low trust, poor UX, and limited adoption. We had to:
Understand the real blockers in daily engineering workflows
Rebuild trust in AI by designing for transparency and usability
Align across execs, engineers, and IT to ship something that sticks
Conducted a three-month research sprint with BMW engineers in Munich
Mapped 37 recurring pain points across 4 engineering personas
Identified bottlenecks in data retrieval, compliance lookup, and cross-department collaboration
Proposed a Copilot-style AI assistant embedded in BMW’s internal toolchain
Positioned it as a productivity booster, not a replacement—framing trust and utility as core design principles
Aligned design with Microsoft’s Human-AI Experience (HAX) framework for ethical, usable AI
Validated 30+ design patterns and narrowed to those with highest adoption potential
Developed natural language interfaces, simple prompt structures, and progressive disclosure of AI results
Worked closely with UX Engineers to build interactive prototypes for real-user testing
Ran iterative testing sessions inside BMW’s Digital Campus and Future Lab
Following the success of 3DC, Microsoft and BMW hosted AI Vision Day (Feb 2025) to explore what comes next.
This 2-day workshop in Munich brought together executives and engineers to define BMW’s future of AI in product development. Highlights included:
Executive panel featuring leaders from BMW and Microsoft
Strategic goal setting: 30% cost reduction, faster go-to-market, and new AI revenue models
Opportunity mapping using 3DC insights as a springboard
Co-design of a next-gen AI engineering assistant with natural language, simplified tone, and built-in transparency
The workshop delivered a prioritised roadmap and prototype concepts—paving the way for BMW’s broader AI transformation.
Delivered the BMW 3DC AI assistant—now used by thousands of engineers to search across parts, documents, and design histories
Reduced search time from hours to seconds in key tasks (e.g. part lookup, design status, compliance checks)
Achieved high pilot engagement across multiple departments
Became a flagship co-innovation project for BMW’s digitalisation strategy
Established a design system and AI interaction model now used in follow-on products
In the future, you will have an AI engineering assistant constantly accompanying your work—it becomes your single interface to everything.
